# corax_mcp_server (Resource)

Manages a Corax MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. MCP servers expose tools, resources, and prompts that can be wired into capabilities.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Copyright (c) Trifork

resource "corax_mcp_server" "corax_data" {
  name = "Corax Data"
  url  = "http://corax-data-mcp-cheetah-application.corax-ai.svc.cluster.local:8000/mcp"
  type = "streamablehttp"

  config = {
    token = {
      type    = "header"
      label   = "Authorization"
      default = null
    }
    filters = {
      type     = "header"
      label    = "X-Filters"
      default  = null
      required = false
    }
    timeZone = {
      type    = "header"
      label   = "X-Time-Zone"
      default = null
    }
    collectionId = {
      type    = "header"
      label   = "X-Collection-Id"
      default = null
    }
    dataProducts = {
      type    = "header"
      label   = "X-Data-Products"
      default = null
    }
    collectionType = {
      type    = "header"
      label   = "X-Collection-Type"
      default = null
    }
    userPreferences = {
      type     = "header"
      label    = "X-User-Preferences"
      default  = null
      required = false
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name for the MCP server. Up to 64 characters.
- `url` (String) URL the Corax backend uses to reach the MCP server.

### Optional

- `config` (String) Server-specific configuration as a JSON-encoded object.
- `type` (String) Transport protocol. One of `streamablehttp` (default) or `sse`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier for the MCP server (UUID).
- `owner` (String) ID of the user that owns the MCP server.
- `slug` (String) URL-safe slug derived from the name.
